---
title: "AI for Coding"
subtitle: "Day 1: Course Overview"
author: |
  <p><br/>**Eduard Bukin** [ebukin@worldbank.org](mailto:ebukin@worldbank.org)
  <br/>Distributional Impact of Policies
  <br/>Fiscal Policy and Growth Department<p/>
date: "April 20, 2024"

# format:
#   revealjs:
#     background-transition: zoom
#     preload-iframes: true
#     smaller: false
#     scrollable: true
#     transition: fade
#     slide-number: true
#     chalkboard:
#       buttons: false
#     preview-links: auto
#     theme: [white, ../../assets/reveal-styles.scss]
#     css: ../../assets/reveal-styles.css
#     logo: ../../assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
#     footer: '[worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding) © 2026 World Bank.'
#     template-partials:
#       - ../../assets/title-slide.html
#     resources:
#       - ai4coding-slides-d1-1.pdf
#       - ../../assets/img/prosp-logo.webp
#       - ../../assets/img/geopov-logo.png
#       - ../../assets/bg-frames/
#       - ../../assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
---

## Welcome

**Goal:** Equip economists with AI-skills for coding.

[2 × 3-hour sessions]{.pill} [Self-study in between]{.pill} [GitHub Copilot · Positron IDE]{.pill} [Stata & modern stat languages]{.pill}


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%" .obj-card .card-understand}
#### Understand

- Core LLM concepts: tokens, prompts, context windows, agents
- How these affect AI-assisted coding
- Strengths and limitations for analytical work
-
:::

::: {.column width="33%" .obj-card .card-apply}
#### Practice

- Use AI to understand, revise and reuse code
- Plan execution and write prompts for multi-step tasks
- Leverage `#tools`, `skills` and `/agents`
- Supervise and course-correct AI-generated code
:::

::: {.column width="33%" .obj-card .card-control}
#### Take Control

- Data-security practices and guardrailing AI.
- Responsible-use guidelines for AI-assisted
:::

::::


## Course Structure

Three parts, each building on the previous one.

::: {.notes}
Emphasise that this is not two standalone sessions — the self-study bridge is essential.
:::

## What to Expect

**Day 1** — See AI in action, learn key concepts, get set up

- Live demo of a real data workflow with AI
- How LLMs work, what they can and cannot do
- Responsible use and data security

**Self-Study** — The most important part

- Your time to build the habit with your own code
- Structured case studies to guide you
- Come back to Day 2 with questions and observations

**Day 2** — Go deeper, work smarter

- Debrief self-study experiences
- Advanced techniques: agents, context engineering, planning
- Hands-on exercises with real workflows

::: {.notes}
Stress that the self-study period is where the real learning happens.
Day 1 gives the foundation; self-study builds muscle memory; Day 2 refines technique.
:::

## Logistics

| | Morning | Evening (repeat) |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | In person + virtual | Virtual only |
| **Session 1** | Mon Apr 20, 9 AM–12 PM ET | Mon Apr 20, 9 PM–12 AM ET |
| **Session 2** | Thu Apr 23, 9 AM–12 PM ET | Thu Apr 23, 9 PM–12 AM ET |

- Materials: [worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding)
- Questions during session: **use Teams chat** or rais hand

::: {.notes}
Point people to the materials site. Mention that everything is there including prerequisites and exercises.
:::
